title: Admiral Ground Truth Document
description: Machine-readable Strategy Triangle: Mission, Boundaries, and Success Criteria. The single source of strategic truth for a project under Admiral governance.

created.description: Date this Ground Truth document was created.

last_reviewed.description: Date this document was last reviewed by a human.

mission.description: Part 1 Mission vertex: what are we building and why.

identity.description: One sentence: what is this project? If you cannot express it in a single sentence, the fleet will not stay aligned.

success_state.description: What must be true for this project to be considered successful? Concrete, observable terms.

stakeholders.description: Who is this for and what core need does it serve?

current_phase.description: Project phase. Changes how agents approach ambiguity, risk, and scope.

pipeline_entry.description: At which Spec-First Pipeline stage the fleet takes over.

boundaries.description: Part 1 Boundaries vertex: what the project is NOT, resource limits, and the LLM-Last line.

non_goals.description: Explicitly state what the project is NOT.

functional.description: Features, capabilities, or user flows explicitly out of scope.

quality.description: Levels of polish, optimization, or completeness not required at this phase.

architectural.description: Patterns, technologies, or approaches that must not be used.

tech_stack.description: Exact languages, frameworks, and tools with version numbers.

deadlines.description: Ship dates, demo dates, review dates.

compatibility.description: Browser support, API backward compatibility, platform requirements.

protocol_scope.description: MCP servers approved for use, A2A requirements, trust classification policy.

resource_budgets.description: Resource limits that prevent unbounded exploration.

token_budget.description: Max tokens per task before the agent must deliver or escalate.

tool_call_limits.description: Max API calls or tool invocations per task.

scope_boundary.description: File paths, repos, and services each agent may touch.

quality_floor.description: Minimum acceptable quality bar, defined concretely.

llm_last.description: The LLM-Last boundary: what deterministic tools handle vs. what requires LLM judgment.

deterministic.description: Tasks handled by static analysis, linters, shell commands, type checkers.

llm_judgment.description: Tasks requiring understanding of intent, tradeoffs, novel design.

success_criteria.description: Part 1 Success Criteria vertex: machine-verifiable definition of done.

functional.description: What must the output DO? Testable behaviors.

completeness.description: What must exist beyond the core deliverable.

negative.description: What the output must NOT do.

failure_guidance.description: What happens when criteria cannot be met. Specify: escalate, deliver partial, or block.

judgment_boundaries.description: Where criteria are ambiguous. Name the ambiguity and assign a decision authority tier.
